incorporations.original: no less easy was it to prove gaining an audience with the man
incorporations.replacement: being presented to the man was not to prove so easy for me
incorporations.reason: Gemini's sentence structure is clearer and more natural. 'Presented' also matches the Italian 'l'essere presentato all'uomo' more faithfully than Claude's 'gaining an audience', while Gemini's own 'introduced' was less literal.

'Wrested' renders Italian 'sottratto' (taken away, wrested) more accurately than 'saved'. 'Avaricious' is closer to Italian 'avaro' than 'miserly'. 'Selfishness' was retained from Claude over Gemini's 'egoism' as it reads more naturally in English.
Italian 'ripose' means 'put away / stored'; Gemini's 'put it away' is more accurate than Claude's 'replaced it', which wrongly implies returning it to a previous location.

incorporations.original: breathed new life into the Motherland
incorporations.replacement: breathed new life into the Fatherland
incorporations.reason: 'Fatherland' is the established English convention for Italian 'Patria' in Risorgimento literature. 'Motherland' skews toward Russian or German associations.
Consistency with the Fatherland/Patria convention adopted in paragraph 8.

incorporations.original: several images moulded into the wall
incorporations.replacement: several pictures pasted upon the wall
incorporations.reason: Italian 'immagini impastate alla parete' means images pasted/plastered onto the wall. Claude's 'moulded into' misreads 'impastate' (pasted) as a sculpting term; Gemini's 'pictures pasted on the wall' is accurate.
